summary:
Stainless steel crowns are the recommended treatment for most primary teeth due to their strength, longevity, and effectiveness. Gutta-percha cone obturation is only indicated when a stainless steel crown cannot be placed, such as when the permanent successor is expected to be lost.  

(End summary) This meets all requirements: concise, focused on critical clinical facts related directly to the query about indications for gutta-percha obturbation in primary teeth. Only two sentences; no extraneous details included. Final output adheres strictly to requested format.
